name: Sing Buri (Thailand)

colors.symbolism: the field of the provincial flag

description: A white field charged with the provincial seal of Sing Buri in the centre.
history: Used as the official flag of Sing Buri province by the provincial government (changwat). Most Thai provincial flags follow a common formula: the provincial seal (the same seal used on the provincial coat of arms) charged on a single-colour field. The designs are traditional and date back to the formation of modern provincial administration in Thailand.

features.symbolism: the field of the provincial flag

the provincial seal — a heraldic emblem depicting iconography (a Buddhist stupa, sacred mountain, river, or other local landmark) characteristic of the province; see the Wikipedia article for details.
